top.title: Flattery and fanfare as Trump welcomed to China - but thorny issues remain
top.summary: The leaders of the world's two superpowers were all smiles for the cameras during the US president's visit to Beijing.

top.title: 'Floating armoury' ship reportedly seized by Iran
top.summary: A vessel said to be a “floating armoury” in the Gulf of Oman has been seized by Iranian military personnel, according to reports.

top.title: Warning of record global temperatures as chance of very strong El Niño grows
top.summary: As El Niño develops this year, scientists are increasingly confident it could be one of the strongest on record with global consequences, as Simon King explains.

top.title: HMRC to use AI from British tech firm to spot fraud and tax return errors
top.summary: Quantexa, a financial data platform, won the £175m contract to spot fraud and tax return errors.

top.title: Thousands of Waymos recalled after robotaxi swept into a creek
top.summary: The voluntary recall follows an incident on 20 April where an empty Waymo car entered a flooded road in San Antonio, Texas.

top.title: WhatsApp launches totally private 'incognito' conversations with its AI chatbot
top.summary: A cyber security expert says deleting chat history could lead to a lack of accountability if things go wrong.
